atomicity.message: Single docs-only change: records owner decision D-OSL-20 authorizing the current-tree scrub and the flip execution window. One file, one purpose.

conditions.message: The staged docs-only decision update is readable Markdown and does not alter or fake lint enforcement.
evidence: CloudyEagle polled Agent Mail and found message 3694 from JadeTower: Spark H3 packet /tmp/pkts-spark-h3.json with response file log/backpressure/responses/cd8ed4e70ebd50bdc66828912cfd1e5f1d798b6c250fc0b2770bc01ee95e96aa.json.
Packet /tmp/pkts-spark-h3.json contains feature diff:docs bound to staged payload hash sha256:cd8ed4e70ebd50bdc66828912cfd1e5f1d798b6c250fc0b2770bc01ee95e96aa.
Current git diff --cached --name-status shows only M docs/decisions-2026-07-02-review-round.md staged; untracked log/backpressure/failed/20260704T215635Z-blocked.json is not staged.
burpvalve hash --staged --json returned sha256:cd8ed4e70ebd50bdc66828912cfd1e5f1d798b6c250fc0b2770bc01ee95e96aa with included_paths [docs/decisions-2026-07-02-review-round.md], matching the packet.
Staged diff adds D-OSL-20 "Flip execution window authorized" with 23 inserted lines in docs/decisions-2026-07-02-review-round.md.
D-OSL-20 records owner authorization on 2026-07-05 for immediate current-tree scrub and the flip execution window, with exact staged launch steps and an explicit fail-closed rule.
backpressure/lint-rules.md says rules are enforced only after a command or analyzer is named. This payload adds no lint command, analyzer, code, or generated lint evidence.
The new prose is a short decision section with clear ordered items and no style-rule-sensitive code surface.

conditions.message: The staged docs-only decision adds one new launch authorization record without duplicated logic, parallel policy implementation, or repeated executable procedure.

git diff --cached --stat reports one modified file with 23 insertions.
The added hunk is a single new section, D-OSL-20. Flip execution window authorized, appended after D-OSL-19.
References to D-OSL-14, D-OSL-5, D-OSL-4, D-OSL-16, D-OSL-7/8, D-OSL-11, and D-OSL-17 are cross-references to existing decisions rather than copied or competing policy text.

conditions.message: The decision records launch authorization while preserving ordered execution and fail-closed escalation, so it does not turn the decision log into a shortcut around gates.
evidence: D-OSL-20 names the authorization date, 2026-07-05, and separates immediate current-tree scrub from the later flip execution window.
The text explicitly says the flip execution window supersedes the D-OSL-5 hold, instead of silently bypassing the prior hard gate.

The execution list remains bound to existing decisions and the ordered launch chain: old release deletion, prefix rename/deferred issue edits, private v0.2.0 launch release, private repo rename, fresh-history public repo creation, About metadata, PVR enablement/verification, visibility flip, and anonymous verification.
The final paragraph says any blocker, verifier disagreement, or unpredicted state stops the sequence and escalates to the owner rather than improvising forward.
The staged change records authorization only; git diff shows no executable gate, release, repo visibility, credential, or remote operation change.
